<Meta title="Atoms/Read Time" />

# Read Time

The Read Time component automatically calculates and displays the estimated reading time for page content. It analyzes the text within the main content area and provides an easy-to-understand time estimate, helping users gauge how long it will take to read through the material.

## Interactive Preview

The Read Time component displays below the sample content. It automatically calculates reading time based on the text length in the main content area.

<div className="sb-contained-canvas">
  <Canvas of={ReadTimeStories.ReadTime} />
</div>

## Technical Specifications

- **Atomic Level**: Atom
- **Template**: `yds-read-time.twig`
- **Styles**: `_yds-read-time.scss`
- **JavaScript**: `yds-read-time.js`

### Calculation Method

The read time is calculated using the following approach:

- **Reading Speed**: 200 words per minute (average adult reading speed)
- **Content Source**: Text within the `#main-content` element
- **Calculation**: Total word count divided by words per minute, rounded up to the nearest minute
- **Minimum Display**: If content is less than 200 words, displays "less than 1 min"

### JavaScript Behavior

The component uses a Drupal behavior that:

1. Locates the `#main-content` element on the page
2. Extracts and cleans the text content (removes extra whitespace)
3. Counts the total number of words
4. Calculates reading time in minutes
5. Updates the display with the calculated time or "less than 1" for short content

### Accessibility

- Semantic HTML structure with clearly labeled time information
- Screen readers announce the complete reading time statement
- Visual and text-based presentation ensures information is accessible to all users
- No interactive elements required - purely informational

### Dependencies

- **Required Element**: The component requires a `#main-content` element to be present on the page
- **JavaScript**: Requires JavaScript to calculate and display the reading time
- **No JavaScript Fallback**: If JavaScript is disabled, the component will show "0 min"

## Usage Guidelines

### Best Practices

- Place the Read Time component at the beginning or end of article content
- Use on long-form content pages like articles, blog posts, and documentation
- Ensure the main content area has the ID `#main-content` for proper calculation
- Keep the default label unless there's a compelling reason to change it
- Position the component in a consistent location across similar content types

### Things to Avoid

- Don't use Read Time on pages with minimal content (less than a paragraph)
- Don't place multiple Read Time components on the same page
- Avoid using on pages without a clearly defined `#main-content` area
- Don't use on highly interactive pages where "reading time" doesn't make sense
- Don't customize the reading speed calculation without research and user testing
- Avoid using on landing pages or marketing pages focused on quick scanning

### When to Use Read Time

- **Long-form articles**: Blog posts, news articles, essays
- **Documentation**: Technical guides, tutorials, how-to content
- **Research publications**: Academic papers, white papers, reports
- **Educational content**: Learning materials, course content
- **Policy documents**: Terms of service, privacy policies, guidelines

### When NOT to Use Read Time

- **Short pages**: Contact forms, error pages, simple landing pages
- **Media-heavy content**: Photo galleries, video pages, interactive media
- **Navigation pages**: Directory listings, search results, sitemaps
- **Dashboards**: Administrative interfaces, data visualizations
- **Transactional pages**: Checkout flows, form submissions

## Implementation Notes

### Content Scope

The Read Time component calculates based on ALL text within the `#main-content` element, including:

- Body text paragraphs
- Headings and subheadings
- List items
- Blockquotes
- Table content
- Any other text elements

### Reading Speed Assumptions

The default 200 words per minute is intentionally on the lower end of average reading speeds to provide a realistic estimate that accounts for:

- Varying reading abilities
- Technical or complex content
- Reading interruptions
- Time spent reviewing images or diagrams

## Testing

For comprehensive visual regression testing with all theme variations, see the [Visreg page](?path=/story/atoms-read-time-visreg--visreg).

## Related Components

- **Text Field**: The primary content component that Read Time measures
- **Breadcrumbs**: Another metadata component often paired with Read Time
- **Byline**: Consider pairing with byline information on article pages
